milestones.status: Code Intel foundation exists and is labeled heuristic; preferred 100K Gate-1 evidence achieved via React target (753,902 LOC indexed); re-export chain call graph correctness fixture present (D-84)
evidence: symbol graph, reference graph, heuristic call graph, and Code Intel tools exist
Gate-1 minimum Vite evidence is present at 86216 supported LOC
Gate-1 preferred React target indexed at 753902 LOC (preferred-100k qualified)
Code Intel descriptions and metadata label heuristic behavior
D67 rename_symbol exposes hashline edit hunks and heuristic confidence metadata
D69 refreshed Gate-1 target inventory and Vite minimum-50k Gate-1 scenario evidence
D71 covers TypeScript combined default-plus-named import references and call edges
D74 resolves TypeScript default re-export barrel call edges to the original named default declaration

D84 records a v1.5 Code Intel re-export chain call-graph fixture: the heuristic correctly recognizes transitive callers across a caller -> intermediate re-exporter -> target boundary (verified by 1 new test in call-graph.test.ts)
blockers: rename_symbol is reference-guided lexical rewrite with hashline edit hunks, not IDE-grade AST rename

milestones.status: memory, Browser, MCP, Automation, and Remote TUI foundations exist; Gate-1.5 binding achieved at 20/20 completed live Browser tasks (D-117 through D-125); Browser enhancement unlocked; D126 adds Browser Tier-1 pure-function foundation; D127 adds explainable Memory Ranking and heuristic Code Intel semantic fallback evidence; D128 adds a machine-readable v2.0 Tier-1 precheck; D129 adds production Browser transcript and visual metadata proof; D130 closes the Compaction Tier-2 row from existing implementation and integration evidence; D131 closes the MCP Runtime Tier-2 row with a one-server stdio JSON-RPC transport proof; D132 closes the Automation Tier-2 row with an injected runner and persisted run-record proof; D133 closes the Remote TUI Tier-2 row with an authenticated injected-transport protocol/session proof
evidence: memory modules exist
Browser foundation modules exist behind opt-in surfaces
MCP package and runtime foundation exist
D70 refreshed Gate-1.5 fixture evidence with binding=false and branchDecision=defer-live-evidence
D73 live Browser task ledger records 0/20 live tasks and binding=false
D114 queues 20 pending candidate live Browser tasks as sourcing evidence only; 0/20 completed live results exist and binding remains false
D115 adds an opt-in live Browser task runner boundary; it runs only with optIn=true plus an injected adapter, and still leaves 0/20 completed live results

D116 adds a pure live Browser result recorder that accepts known task results, ignores unknown and duplicate rows, and delegates binding to the required 20 completed-result threshold while repository evidence remains 0/20

D117 adds the opt-in live Browser evidence runner that orchestrates the chain end-to-end (queue D-114 + runner D-115 + recorder D-116) and records the first opt-in run with a stub adapter, advancing the repository ledger from 0/20 to 1/20 completed live results; binding remains false because 19/20 are still pending

D118 adds the opt-in live Browser evidence batch runner that calls the D-117 single-run chain `batchSize` times in a loop with the updated ledger between iterations, advancing the repository ledger from 1/20 to 4/20 (3 more) via a single batch call with a stub adapter; binding remains false because 16/20 are still pending

D119 adds the real HTTP Browser evidence adapter that uses Node's built-in `fetch` (HTTP only, no JS execution) to record real network-call evidence for 2 candidate tasks, advancing the repository ledger from 4/20 to 6/20 (2 more real-evidence tasks); of the 6 cumulative completed live results, 4 are stub-evidence (D-117/118) and 2 are real-evidence (D-119); binding remains false because 14/20 are still pending; D-119 adds zero new npm deps

D120 adds the hybrid real Browser evidence runner that records 2 additional HTTP-evidence tasks plus 1 JS-evidence task, advancing the repository ledger from 6/20 to 9/20; cumulative evidence is 4 stub + 4 HTTP + 1 JS result, and binding remains false because 11/20 are still pending

D121 fixes hybrid evidence accumulation for non-contiguous mapped pending tasks so future runs can skip unmapped pending rows without aborting; this keeps repository evidence at 9/20 and binding=false

D122 adds optional per-task JS action mapping to recordHybridRealBrowserEvidence so remaining JS evidence can distinguish fill-search-input, click-element, and extract-text actions; this keeps repository evidence at 9/20 and binding=false

D123 makes recordHybridRealBrowserEvidence return a recomputed updatedLedger and recalculates binding/branchDecision through the live Browser ledger builder; this keeps repository evidence at 9/20 and binding=false

D124 chains two updatedLedger accumulation runs of recordHybridRealBrowserEvidence to record 4 additional live evidence results (cart-update-quantity as HTTP-evidence from https://example.com/, checkout-address-validation as JS click-element on https://example.com/, table-filter as HTTP-evidence from https://www.iana.org/, and table-pagination as JS extract-text on https://www.iana.org/), advancing the repository ledger from 9/20 to 13/20; cumulative evidence is 4 stub + 6 HTTP + 3 JS, and binding remains false because 7/20 are still pending

D125 completes the final 7 live Browser tasks (settings-toggle, profile-edit, modal-open-close, tabs-switch, breadcrumb-navigation, download-link-detection, error-page-recovery) via real HTTP fetch evidence, advancing the repository ledger from 13/20 to 20/20; binding becomes true and browserEnhancementUnlocked becomes true

D126 implements the first Browser Tier-1 foundation slice as pure TypeScript helpers: richer semantic DOM extraction, pageSummary, deterministic rankElementsForIntent candidates with reasons/scores, bounded action history, repeated-action detection, and planner selection that avoids repeating the same target when alternatives exist (verified by Browser unit tests)

D127 adds explainable Memory Ranking and deterministic Code Intel semantic fallback evidence: rankMemoriesWithScores and MemoryStore.rank expose score factors/reasons, semantic-index results expose matched tokens/coverage/reason/stable tie-breaking, and smart_search local/all reports heuristic semantic_fallback results for free-text queries while exact symbol matches remain higher priority

D128 adds a machine-readable v2.0 Tier-1 precheck: Browser helper evidence, Memory Ranking evidence, Code Intel semantic fallback evidence, and default-exposure invariants pass; production Browser automation, visual grounding, and Tier-2 blockers remain blocked

D129 adds a production Browser proof recorder with an injected adapter contract: the evidence records an ordered navigate/type/click/observe/visual-snapshot transcript, validates visual snapshot metadata, keeps raw screenshot bytes out of repository evidence, and leaves default exposure unchanged

D130 closes the v2.0 Tier-2 Compaction row using existing implementation and integration evidence: core session compaction, agent tool-loop compaction, print/RPC compaction configuration paths, lifecycle hook tests, and cross-protocol compaction smoke coverage

D131 closes the v2.0 Tier-2 MCP Runtime row with a one-server stdio JSON-RPC transport proof: coding-agent starts the gh-search MCP server, completes initialize/tools-list/tools-call, registers discovered tools behind the opt-in mcp profile, and keeps the default registry unchanged

D132 closes the v2.0 Tier-2 Automation row with an injected runner execution proof: enabled CronStore jobs execute through AutomationRuntime, disabled jobs are skipped, success and failed outcomes are persisted to cron/runs.json, and one failed job does not stop later enabled jobs

D133 closes the v2.0 Tier-2 Remote TUI row with an authenticated injected-transport protocol/session proof: hello handshakes authenticate clients, unauthorized clients are rejected before input forwarding, input and resize events reach injected local handlers, output frames are sequenced, and close behavior is deterministic
blockers: default registry must remain coding plus Code Intel essentials
D129 proves the production Browser adapter contract and visual-grounding metadata path, but it is not a live external Browser run
D133 proves the Remote TUI core protocol/session bridge, not a full WebSocket server, browser UI, TLS/auth stack, reconnect layer, or hosted app-server

milestones.status: planner/DAG/cache modules exist; main-loop integration has multi-scenario planner.plan evidence fixtures (D-77 + 3 D-81 + D-82); v5 implementation gate reached (v2.5 >= 65%)

release gate scenarios are not freshly proven in this scorecard

D134 records a v3/v4 production precheck: Gate-2 live fixture and Reviewer gate boundary evidence pass, but v3 production breadth remains blocked until multi-scenario long-horizon replay evidence exists

milestones.status: Researcher, TaskGraph, memory, and channel foundations exist; cross-session memory crash/reload evidence plus multi-hop cross-session TaskGraph persistence evidence fixtures present; D134 records these in the v3/v4 production precheck while keeping cross-platform SIGKILL blocked
evidence: Researcher and TaskGraph modules exist
persistent memory and channel foundation modules exist
D78 records atomic write semantics plus partial-last-line recovery for the persistent memory store
D80 records cross-session TaskGraph persistence: a second PersistingTaskGraphRecorder instance loaded from the same file sees the first instance's records (D-78 storage layer + D-80 Agent OS layer)

D86 records multi-hop cross-session handoff: 3 instances (A writes 3, B writes 2, C reads all 5 in order) verify the JSONL format supports Agent OS session handoffs with multiple intermediate instances
D134 records a v3/v4 production precheck: v4 cross-session Agent OS and persistent memory recovery evidence pass, but cross-platform SIGKILL/restore remains blocked
blockers: Agent OS orchestration is not production-complete
Desktop and channels remain stopped or opt-in, not default-enabled
cross-session crash recovery evidence is deterministic unit-style fixtures (D-78 + D-80 + D-86), not real cross-platform SIGKILL tests

caveats: Gate-2 default-profile fixture pass is not v1-v4 production completion.
Gate-1 minimum-50k evidence is not preferred-100k evidence.
Module existence is not production integration.
Code Intel rename, reference, call graph, and smart search behavior remains heuristic unless a specific test proves stronger semantics.
Default registry exposure remains coding plus Code Intel essentials; non-coding surfaces require explicit opt-in.
nextActions: D135: record multi-scenario v3.0 production long-horizon replay evidence without expanding default exposure.
Continue preferred-100k Gate-1 search only when a local 100K+ target is available.
Keep Gate-2 production, cross-platform Desktop, and cross-platform SIGKILL evidence as separate future blockers rather than inferring them from unit fixtures.
